codeReview.summary: The direction-map.html implementation fully covers all feature requirements: all 4 directions from jade_dashboard.json are rendered in a sortable table with name, heat, confidence, judgment state (color-coded badge), classification, audience fit, and price band columns. Three sort controls (heat, confidence, name) and five filter controls (all, 值得跟, 观察中, 短热噪音, 需要补证据) are implemented and functional. Click navigation to direction-detail.html?id=<id> works via both row click and name link. Navigation bar correctly highlights 方向地图 via nav.js auto-detection. All text is in Chinese. The implementation makes good use of shared JS modules (data.js, components.js, nav.js) and CSS design system custom properties. Code quality is solid for a prototype — clean structure, proper event handling, responsive design with 3 breakpoints, and an empty-state message when filters yield no results.

issues.description: Stats summary grid shows counts for 值得跟, 观察中, and 短热噪音 but omits 需要补证据. The data has 1 direction (dir_003 沉香+翡翠叠戴) with judgment_state '需要补证据', and a filter button for it exists, but the stat box is missing. This creates a minor inconsistency — the filter bar has 5 options but the stats grid only shows 4 boxes (total + 3 states).

Direction data fields (dir.id, dir.name, dir.audience_fit, dir.price_band_fit) are interpolated directly into HTML via template literals without sanitization. Safe for a prototype with local JSON data, but worth noting as a pattern concern if the data source ever changes.

The onclick handler uses single-quoted string interpolation: onclick="navigateToDetail('${dir.id}')". If a direction ID ever contained a single quote, this would break. Current IDs (dir_001-dir_004) are safe, but the pattern is fragile.

summary: Direction Map page passes review. The implementation fully satisfies all feature requirements: sortable/filterable table of all directions with correct data fields, color-coded judgment badges, click navigation to detail pages, active navigation highlighting, and all-Chinese UI. Three non-blocking issues noted: missing 需要补证据 stat box in the summary grid, unsanitized HTML interpolation (acceptable for prototype), and fragile single-quote pattern in onclick handler. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly, reused shared modules effectively, and verified the implementation thoroughly with agent-browser and pytest.
